text: Motorcycle (boda-boda) repair is a high-demand business across Uganda with over 1 million bodas on the road. Startup requirements: basic tool set (spanners, screwdrivers, pliers, jack: UGX 200,000-400,000), workspace (roadside or rented shade: UGX 50,000-150,000/month), initial spare parts stock (UGX 300,000-500,000 for common items like brake pads, cables, chains, spark plugs). Total startup: UGX 600,000-1,200,000. Revenue: basic service/tune-up charges UGX 20,000-30,000, major repairs UGX 50,000-150,000. A busy mechanic can earn UGX 600,000-1,500,000/month.

Growing your boda repair business: 1) Start with basic services (oil changes, brake adjustments, tire repairs) and gradually add electrical work, engine rebuilds. 2) Build relationships with boda-boda stages — become their preferred mechanic. 3) Offer a simple warranty: 'If my repair fails within 1 week, I fix it free.' This builds trust and repeat customers. 4) Stock the most common spare parts to avoid sending customers elsewhere. 5) Consider training apprentices (youth from the community) — they help you handle more customers while learning a trade. 6) Keep a record book of all jobs for tracking and accountability.
